subject: Mathematics
title: Check Digits and Error Detection
description: A 60-second lesson on Check Digits and Error Detection.

hook.text: Type one wrong digit in a credit card number and the system catches it instantly.

buildup.text: Credit cards use the Luhn algorithm, a checksum based on modular arithmetic.

discovery.text: ISBN book codes use mod 11. If the check digit doesn't match, the code is rejected.

twist.text: These systems catch about 95% of single-digit errors with just one extra number.

climax.text: Every barcode, bank account, and ID number quietly runs modular math behind the scenes.

punchline.text: One extra digit catches almost every mistake.

quiz.question: What mathematical concept do check digits rely on?
